---
title: "From Digital Trace Data to Communication Styles"
subtitle: "A Generalized Multimodal Video Analysis Framework for Audience-Side Linkage"
author:
  - name: "Mu-Jung Cho | 卓牧融"
    orcid: '0000-0001-5500-6336'
    email: 'mjcho@as.edu.tw'
    affiliations:
      - name: 'RCHSS, Academia Sinica'
title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-image: https://media1.tenor.com/m/TmIXaAyzuwwAAAAd/space-art-stars-cosmic.gif
    data-background-opacity: "0.2"
format:
  revealjs:
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    theme: [default, synthwave84-reveal-2.scss]
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.06
    slide-number: c
    incremental: true
    slide-level: 2
    embed-resources: true
    # scrollable is now per-slide opt-in (add {.scrollable} to a heading)
    # rather than global, so reveal auto-fits dense slides instead of scrolling.
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  error: false
occassion: "CMM Special Issue — Proposal Presentation"
date: last-modified
editor:
  render-on-save: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
# minimal setup chunk
```

```{r}
#| label: title-bg
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

# --- Dynamic title background -------------------------------------------
# Leave NULL for a random pick; set to a filename in ../BG to override.
bg_choice <- NULL          # e.g. "sunset_grid.png"

bg_dir   <- "../BG"
bg_files <- list.files(bg_dir, pattern = "\\.(png|jpe?g|gif|webp)$",
                       ignore.case = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

if (length(bg_files) > 0) {
  pick <- if (is.null(bg_choice)) {
    bg_files[sample.int(length(bg_files), 1)]   # random
  } else {
    file.path(bg_dir, bg_choice)                # override
  }

  if (file.exists(pick)) {
    uri <- xfun::base64_uri(pick)   # -> "data:image/png;base64,...."
    cat(paste0(
      '<script>(function(){',
      'var u="', uri, '";',
      'function apply(){',
        'var t=document.getElementById("title-slide");',
        'if(!t)return false;',
        't.setAttribute("data-background-image",u);',
        't.setAttribute("data-background-size","cover");',
        't.setAttribute("data-background-position","center");',
        't.setAttribute("data-background-opacity","0.1");',
        'if(window.Reveal&&Reveal.sync)Reveal.sync();',
        'return true;};',
      'if(window.Reveal&&Reveal.isReady&&Reveal.isReady()){apply();}',
      'else{document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded",function(){',
        'var n=0,iv=setInterval(function(){',
          'if((window.Reveal&&Reveal.isReady&&Reveal.isReady()&&apply())||++n>100)',
          'clearInterval(iv);},50);});}',
      '})();</script>'
    ))
  }
}
```

# The Measurement Problem

---

## Why Content Matters More Than Screen Time

* Media effects research must move beyond [aggregate use time]{.neon-green} to study the specific content users actually interact with.

* The modern media environment is [fragmented, personalized, and fast-lived]{.neon-green} — traditional methods cannot keep up.

* Social media video epitomizes this challenge: it is inherently [multimodal]{.neon-cyan}, conveying meaning through visual cues, vocal performance, and spoken text simultaneously.

* We need integrated computational approaches that can handle this complexity.

[Pouwels et al. (2024); Ohme et al. (2024); Kroon et al. (2024).]{.citation}

---

# The CMM Three-Step Framework

---

## Content-Based Media Effects Research

### Pouwels et al. (2024)

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

Three steps for computational media effects:

1. **Collect** digital trace data (DTD) via APIs, data donation, or tracking.
2. **Analyze** content with automated textual and visual methods.
3. **Link** content features to individual-level outcomes.
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**What's been done:**

* Producer-side validation — U.S. Senate campaign videos (Chang et al.; Hsiao et al., under review).

**What's missing:**

* [Audience-side application]{.neon-cyan} — what real audiences *consume*, linked to survey outcomes at the individual level.
:::
::::

---

## From Producer Side to Audience Side

* The validated producer-side pipeline relies on [speaker-specific]{.neon-green} preprocessing (face detection, diarization, ASR).

* Audience consumption spans [diverse, multi-creator corpora]{.neon-green} — no single target speaker.

* We need a [generalized pipeline]{.neon-cyan} that:
  * operates without speaker-reference templates
  * scales to heterogeneous video content
  * generates representations rich enough for theoretically motivated linkage

* This paper introduces and validates such a framework.

---

# Pipeline Architecture

---

## Two-Track Design — Overview {.scrollable}

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-pipeline
%%| fig-cap: "Two-track multimodal video analysis pipeline. Track A structured features (teal) are reserved for post-hoc SHAP interpretation only. Track B semantic embeddings (purple) drive UMAP + HDBSCAN clustering."
%%| out-width: "85%"

%%{init: {
  "theme": "base",
  "flowchart": {"nodeSpacing": 28, "rankSpacing": 36, "diagramPadding": 8},
  "themeVariables": {
    "primaryColor": "#f0efeb",
    "primaryTextColor": "#1a1a1a",
    "primaryBorderColor": "#b0aea6",
    "lineColor": "#666666",
    "edgeLabelBackground": "#ffffff",
    "fontFamily": "Roboto, sans-serif",
    "fontSize": "16px"
  }
}}%%

flowchart TD
    IN["TCS2023 YouTube watch history
    Stratified sample ~30 videos/participant
    Availability check · (+ TCS2022 robustness)"]

    IN --> SA ; IN --> SB

    SA["Track A — Structured features
    Gemini Flash JSON · ~80 features, 5 domains
    + prosodics if speech confirmed"]:::teal

    SB["Track B — Semantic embeddings
    Gemini Flash desc. → Gemini Embedding 2
    desc. 1024D + video 1024D = 2048D"]:::purple

    SA -.->|"post-hoc only"| SH
    SB --> UM

    UM["UMAP dimensionality reduction
    n_components ∈ [5,30] tuned by Optuna
    2D projection separate (viz only)"]

    UM --> CL

    CL["HDBSCAN clustering
    Optuna: n_components · n_neighbors ·
    min_cluster_size · 5 quality metrics"]

    CL --> ST

    ST["Stability check
    ARI across 20 seeds · threshold ≥ 0.70"]:::amber

    ST --> EX ; ST --> SH

    EX["Exemplar selection
    hdbscan.exemplars_"]:::coral

    SH["SHAP classifier stack
    CatBoost · LightGBM · NB baseline
    Per-cluster feature importance"]:::coral

    EX --> LB ; SH --> LB

    LB["LLM cluster labeling
    Top SHAP features + exemplar descriptions"]

    LB --> TY

    TY["Video typology
    Per-participant exposure profiles"]

    TY --> LA

    LA["OLS linkage analysis · TCS2023
    Selective exposure (D21–D22)
    Civic participation (B31–B32)
    Threat perception (G01–G06)"]:::blue

    classDef teal fill:#e8f3f0,stroke:#1a6b5a,stroke-width:1.5px,color:#0f2e26
    classDef purple fill:#eeedfe,stroke:#534AB7,stroke-width:1.5px,color:#26215C
    classDef coral fill:#faece7,stroke:#993C1D,stroke-width:1px,color:#4A1B0C
    classDef blue fill:#e6f1fb,stroke:#185FA5,stroke-width:1px,color:#042C53
    classDef amber fill:#faeeda,stroke:#BA7517,stroke-width:1px,color:#412402
```

---

## Track A — Structured Feature Extraction

### Gemini Flash JSON Schema → ~80 Features

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

Replaces speaker-centric preprocessing with a [standardized JSON schema]{.neon-green}:

1. **Basic metadata** — duration, aspect ratio
2. **Visual scene & style** — setting, lighting, production quality, crowd level
3. **Human presence** — count, gender presentation, framing
4. **Text & branding** — subtitles, logos, slogans
5. **Audio-content** — speech presence, music type, language
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

* For videos with confirmed speech, [diagnostic prosodic features]{.neon-green} are additionally extracted:
  * fundamental frequency (F0)
  * speech rate
  * vocal intensity (MFCC0)
  * vocal emotion

* Preserves continuity with prior pipeline's feature vocabulary.

[Bridges the "sensory gap" (Peng et al., 2024).]{.citation}
:::
::::

---

## Track B — Semantic Embeddings

### LMM Description + Video → Concatenated 2048D Vector

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

* Gemini Flash generates a [natural language description]{.neon-green} per video.

* Both description and raw video are embedded via [Gemini Embedding 2]{.neon-green}:
  * Description embedding: 1,024D
  * Video embedding: 1,024D
  * Concatenated: [2,048D]{.neon-cyan}

* Same encoder ensures dimensional and distributional compatibility.
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Why concatenation, not joint single-pass?**

* A joint pass collapses two independent 1,024D representations into one — [discarding complementary information]{.neon-cyan}.

* Concatenation preserves both and lets the [downstream clustering algorithm]{.neon-green} learn the cross-modal weighting.

[Extended from Cerit et al. (2025) multimodal screen content analysis.]{.citation}
:::
::::

---

# Clustering & Interpretation

---

## UMAP + HDBSCAN + Optuna

### Multi-Metric Clustering Pipeline

* [UMAP applied twice]{.neon-green}:
  * High-dimensional (n_components ∈ [5, 30]) → HDBSCAN input
  * 2D → visualization only

* [Optuna]{.neon-green} simultaneously optimizes five quality metrics:
  * Silhouette score, DBCV, Calinski-Harabasz, Davies-Bouldin, noise ratio

* Search space: `n_components`, `n_neighbors`, `min_dist`, `min_cluster_size`, `min_samples`

* **Critical design choice:** Track A structured features are [excluded from clustering input]{.neon-cyan} entirely — reserved for post-hoc interpretation only.

---

## Why Exclude Structured Features from Clustering?

* Adding heterogeneous structured features risks [degrading UMAP's manifold estimation]{.neon-green} with noise from variables already implicitly encoded in the 2,048D semantic-visual representation.

* More fundamentally, it [conflates two analytically distinct operations]{.neon-cyan}:
  * **Finding** natural video groupings (embedding-space structure)
  * **Explaining** those groupings (human-interpretable terms)

* Keeping structured features post-hoc yields a clean question:

  > Given clusters that formed on their own, [which observable features best discriminate them?]{.neon-cyan}

---

## Cluster Interpretation {.tight}

### SHAP Classifier Stack + LLM Labeling

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Exemplar selection:**

* HDBSCAN has no centroids — use `exemplars_` (highest-density members of each cluster's condensed tree).

**SHAP classifier stack:**

* [CatBoost]{.neon-green} (primary) — handles categorical features natively
* [LightGBM]{.neon-green} (comparison) — leaf-wise tree growth
* [Complement NB]{.neon-green} (baseline) — confirms interactions beyond marginal frequencies
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Robustness logic:**

* Where CatBoost and LightGBM [agree]{.neon-cyan} on top SHAP features → importance is [robust]{.neon-cyan}.
* Divergences → flag instability for investigation.

**LLM labeling:**

* Input: per-cluster SHAP feature profile + exemplar descriptions
* Output: [data-driven, human-readable]{.neon-green} cluster names and profiles

**Stability check:**

* 20 random seeds → pairwise ARI
* Threshold: [mean ARI ≥ 0.70]{.neon-cyan}
:::
::::

---

# Data & Sampling

---

## TCS2023 Digital Trace Study {.smaller-content}

### 125 Participants · 76,325 YouTube Records

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

* **Source:** 2023 Taiwan Communication Survey (Chang, 2025)

* YouTube watch history + four-wave surveys:
  * Political participation
  * Selective exposure behavior
  * Cross-strait threat perception
  * Political affect

* Three-month panel spanning the [2024 Taiwanese presidential election]{.neon-green} campaign period.
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Sampling strategy:**

* ~30 videos/participant via [frequency-weighted]{.neon-green} random selection
  * Probability ∝ within-participant watch count
  * Habitually consumed content preferentially represented

* Deleted/unavailable videos → replaced by next available

* Minimum [30 available videos]{.neon-cyan} per participant required

* Target: [~3,750 videos]{.neon-cyan} total

* TCS2022 (Chang, 2023) for cross-year robustness.
:::
::::

---

# Linkage Analysis

---

## Exploratory Proof-of-Concept, Not Confirmatory

* Cluster labels are [unknown prior to pipeline execution]{.neon-cyan} — we cannot pre-specify variable names.

* All [k−1 cluster proportions]{.neon-green} (one dropped as reference baseline) enter each regression simultaneously.

* Theory provides interpretive [priors]{.neon-cyan}, not confirmatory hypotheses.

* Significant coefficients are interpreted post-hoc using LLM-assigned cluster labels.

* Diet diversity: [HHI]{.neon-green} = 1 − Σ p~k~^2^ (higher = more even distribution across cluster types).

* All models: `lm_robust(..., se_type = "CR2", clusters = pid)`

---

## Three Theoretical Priors → Six OLS Models {.tight}

### Individual-Level, Theory-Guided Linkage

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Prior 1 — Selective exposure**
[(Stroud, 2011)]{.citation}

* Higher HHI + passive consumption breadth → lower active partisan selectivity
* DVs: D21 (pro-attitude engagement, 0–5), D22 (avoidance behavior, 0–5)

**Prior 2 — Media mobilization**
[(Norris, 2000; Livingstone & Markham, 2008)]{.citation}

* News/public affairs cluster exposure → higher civic participation
* DVs: B31 (online civic activities, 0–7), B32 (offline activities, 0–7)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Prior 3 — Cultivation of threat**
[(Gerbner & Gross, 1976; Omar & Casero-Ripollés, 2023)]{.citation}

* Military/security cluster exposure → higher cross-strait threat perception
* DVs: `threat_index` (mean std G01–G05), `defense_support` (G06 count, 0–7)

**Shared covariates:**

* log(youtube_weekly_hrs)
* political interest (B21)
* party identification (E11–E13)
:::
::::

---

# Timeline & Contributions

---

## Project Timeline (Jun–Sep 2026) {.smaller-content}

| Period | Phase | Activities |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Jun 2026 | Setup & data prep | IRB extension; TCS data access; sampling frame design; Gemini Flash JSON schema; parallel API config. |
| Jul 2026 | Pipeline execution | Track A: structured features (~3,750 videos, ~2–3 weeks). Track B: LMM descriptions + Embedding 2 concatenation (2048D). Archive to Parquet. |
| Aug 2026 | Analysis | Optuna UMAP+HDBSCAN. ARI stability (20 seeds). CatBoost + LightGBM + NB SHAP. LLM labeling. Six OLS models. TCS2022 robustness. |
| Sep 2026 | Writing & submission | Manuscript draft; co-author review; submit to CMM. Preprint to OSF/SocArXiv. Open-source pipeline release (MIT). |

---

## Contributions

* A [generalized framework]{.neon-green} solving three interconnected methodological challenges:
  * Extracting meaningful features from diverse, creator-agnostic video corpora
  * Constructing interpretable content typologies from hybrid representations
  * Conducting [individual-level linkage]{.neon-cyan} connecting video consumption to communication outcomes

* Demonstrates that the CMM three-step framework extends from [political producer communication]{.neon-green} to [everyday audience consumption]{.neon-cyan}.

* Provides a [replicable, open-source pipeline]{.neon-green} equipped to generate new, data-driven theories of multimodal media effects.

---

## Thank You

### Questions?

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

This framework moves audience-side media effects research from coarse screen time measures to [content-aware, individual-level linkage analysis]{.neon-green}.

Pipeline code and structured feature schema will be released as [open-source tools]{.neon-cyan} alongside the paper.
:::
::: {.column}

#### Mu-Jung 'MJ' Cho | 卓牧融
[mjcho@as.edu.tw]{.neon-red}

RCHSS, Academia Sinica

:::
::::
